errors.wasm.title: Failed to Initialize Music Engine
errors.wasm.offline_body: This app requires one online visit to download the music engine before offline mode works.
errors.wasm.offline_reload: Please connect to the internet and reload the page.
errors.wasm.generic_body: Your browser may not support WebAssembly, or there was an error loading the music engine.
errors.wasm.offline_note: After one online visit, all features work offline.
errors.wasm.browser_hint: Try using a modern browser like Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge.

ios_install.title: Install Graditone
ios_install.step_intro: Install this app on your iPad for the best experience:
ios_install.step_add: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
android_install.title: Graditone is on Google Play
android_install.subtitle: Install the app for the best experience
offline.banner: You're offline — all features work normally
guide.what.heading: What is Graditone?
guide.what.p1: Graditone is a tablet-native app for interactive music scores, designed for practice and performance. Load any MusicXML score, hear it played back with real-time note highlighting, and train note-by-note with MIDI-driven step practice or ear-training exercises.
guide.what.p2: Graditone works fully offline — once loaded, no internet connection is required.
guide.what.p3: Follow Graditone on Bluesky for the latest project news and updates.
guide.play.heading: Playing a Score
guide.play.intro: Open the Play plugin (🎼) to load and play a score.
guide.play.tap_note: Tap a note — seek playback to that note and highlight it.
guide.play.longpress_note: Long-press a note — pin it (green highlight); sets the loop start point.
guide.play.longpress_second: Long-press a second note — define a loop region between the two pinned notes.
guide.play.tap_loop: Tap inside the loop region — clear the loop and return to free playback.
guide.play.tempo: Use the tempo slider in the playback toolbar to slow down or speed up playback for practice.
guide.practice.heading: Practice Mode
guide.practice.intro: Open the Practice plugin to practise a score note by note.
guide.practice.step1: Select a score from the library.
guide.practice.step2: Connect your MIDI keyboard (USB or Bluetooth) to your device.
guide.practice.step3: Tap Practice — the first target note is highlighted on the staff.
guide.practice.step4: Play the highlighted note on your MIDI keyboard to advance. The app waits until you play the correct pitch.
guide.practice.step5: Continue until the end of the score. Your progress is shown in the toolbar.
guide.practice.freeplay: Practice also works without a loaded score — play freely and see each detected note displayed on the staff in real time.
guide.train.heading: Train
guide.train.intro: Open the Train plugin (🎹) to sharpen your note-reading with ear-training exercises.
guide.train.complexity: Complexity levels — tap one to preset tempo, note count, and mode:
guide.train.low: Low — 8 notes · C4 scale · Step mode · 40 BPM
guide.train.level_remembered: Your selected level is remembered across sessions.
guide.train.step: Step — the app waits for you to play the correct note before advancing.
guide.train.flow: Flow — the exercise plays through in real time; you play along and receive a score at the end.
guide.train.random: Random — random notes from the selected clef and octave range.
guide.train.c4scale: C4 Scale — notes from the C4 major scale (good for beginners).
guide.train.score_preset: Score — notes extracted from the currently loaded score.
guide.train.input: Input sources: Connect a MIDI keyboard (USB or Bluetooth) for best pitch accuracy. Alternatively, use the device microphone for acoustic instruments.
guide.loading.heading: Loading a Score
guide.loading.formats: Graditone loads MusicXML files (.mxl, .musicxml, .xml) — the industry-standard open format exported by MuseScore (free), Sibelius, Finale, Dorico, and hundreds of other notation apps.
guide.loading.preloaded: Preloaded demo scores — no upload needed. Tap Load score inside the Play or Train plugin to choose from the built-in library: Bach Invention No. 1, Beethoven Für Elise, Burgmuller Arabesque, Chopin Nocturne Op. 9 No. 2, and Pachelbel Canon in D.
guide.loading.step1: Export your score as MusicXML from your notation software (in MuseScore: File → Export → MusicXML).
guide.loading.step2: In the Play or Train plugin, tap Load score → Load from file….
guide.loading.step3: Select the .mxl or .musicxml file from your device.
guide.loading.step4: The score loads immediately and is saved in the browser — it will be available next time you open Graditone, even offline.
guide.loading.sources: Free MusicXML scores are available at musescore.com, imslp.org, and kern.humdrum.org.
